skill.name: widgets-ui
skill.description: Build React and Next.js interfaces from declarative widget data. The skill documents a WidgetRenderer pattern for forms, cards, layouts, inputs, selects, buttons, badges, images, and action handling.
skill.summary: Render structured agent responses as interactive React and Next.js widgets.
skill.author: inferen-sh

security_audit.summary: The high weak-cryptography alerts are false positives; SKILL.md contains no cryptographic implementation or weak hash usage. Most external-command alerts are Markdown fences or TypeScript and JSON examples, but the skill does recommend npx commands that fetch remote registries and skills, which creates a legitimate supply-chain warning for users.

medium_findings.title: Remote Installation Commands
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to run npx commands that fetch code or skill packages from remote sources. This is a normal setup pattern, but users should inspect the fetched registry and package contents before running the commands in a project.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and invoke npx with remote package or skill identifiers. The surrounding context is installation documentation, so this is a supply-chain concern rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: External Documentation and Image Links
low_findings.description: The skill links to external documentation, an image asset, a registry URL, and a placeholder image URL. These links are expected for documentation, but they require network access if users open them or install the referenced registry.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible in Markdown documentation and examples. I did not find evidence that the skill itself performs automatic network requests.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives
low_findings.description: The Ruby backtick execution and weak cryptography alerts are not supported by the file content. The matched lines are Markdown fences, descriptive text, table separators, or examples, not executable Ruby code or cryptographic functions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: SKILL.md is documentation and no Ruby execution syntax or weak cryptographic algorithm call appears in the inspected lines. The evidence matches formatting and examples rather than executable implementation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote Registry Fetch Through npx
dangerous_patterns.description: The quick start asks users to add a shadcn registry from an external URL. This can modify project files with remote content and should be reviewed before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command directly references a remote registry URL. The risk is clear, although the documented purpose appears legitimate.

content.user_title: Build Widget UIs from Agent Output
content.value_statement: Teams need a repeatable way to turn structured agent responses into usable interfaces. This skill shows how to render declarative widget data as React and Next.js forms, cards, layouts, and actions.
actual_capabilities: Documents installation of a WidgetRenderer component through a shadcn registry command.
Shows how to render text, labels, captions, titles, badges, icons, images, and dividers.
Describes row, column, and box layouts with spacing, alignment, padding, radius, and gradients.
Provides examples for buttons, inputs, textareas, selects, checkboxes, and form submission.
Explains action handling for click and submit events with optional form data.
limitations: It is documentation only and does not include the component source inside this skill file.
Users must trust and review the external registry before installing generated code.
The examples target React and Next.js projects that use compatible import aliases.
It does not define backend validation, persistence, authentication, or data fetching behavior.

use_cases.title: Render Agent Responses
use_cases.description: Convert structured assistant output into interactive cards, summaries, and controls inside a React application.

use_cases.title: Prototype Dynamic Forms
use_cases.description: Create form layouts from widget data before committing to a custom component design.

use_cases.title: Standardize Generated UI
use_cases.description: Give product teams a shared widget vocabulary for layouts, typography, actions, and display elements.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Simple Widget
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a basic rendered UI.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a simple widgets-ui example for a status card with a title, body text, badge, and one button.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Form Widget
prompt_templates.scenario: Build an interactive form from structured fields.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use widgets-ui to design a contact form with name, email, message, consent checkbox, and submit handling.

prompt_templates.title: Model an Agent Response
prompt_templates.scenario: Transform an assistant result into a user interface.
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this agent response into a widgets-ui layout with clear sections, actions, and readable display components.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Widget Schema
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this widgets-ui structure for accessibility, action handling, form data names, layout consistency, and safe image usage.

output_examples.input: Create a flight summary widget for a user booking confirmation.
output_examples.output: A compact flight card with departure and arrival columns, a travel icon, route details, and a confirmation action.

output_examples.input: Create a support contact form using widgets-ui.
output_examples.output: A vertical form with name, email, message, consent checkbox, and a submit button that returns form data.

output_examples.input: Turn a product recommendation into an interactive card.
output_examples.output: A product card with title, image, price badge, description text, and buttons for viewing details or saving the item.

best_practices: Review external registry content before running the npx installation command.
Validate all form data on the server even when widgets define field names and controls.
Keep widget schemas small, explicit, and aligned with accessible labels and image alt text.
anti_patterns: Do not render untrusted widget data without validating allowed widget types and properties.
Do not place secrets, tokens, or private URLs inside widget definitions.
Do not rely on client-side widget actions as authorization or persistence logic.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps build React and Next.js interfaces from declarative widget data generated by agents or application logic.

faq.question: Does the skill include component source code?
faq.answer: No. The skill documents installation and usage patterns for components fetched from an external shadcn registry.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, and Claude Code use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is this only for forms?
faq.answer: No. It also covers cards, layout containers, text, badges, icons, images, and action handling.

faq.question: What is the main security consideration?
faq.answer: The main concern is the remote npx installation command. Review the fetched registry content before using it.

faq.question: Does it handle backend processing?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on UI rendering and client-side action callbacks, not backend validation or persistence.
